exemplar.title: To what extent do SHEINâs environmental sustainability practices influence its market performance in the Philippines?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent do SHEINâs environmental sustainability practices influence its market performance in the Philippines?

subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: Consistent framing of investigation around ethics across major analytical tools
Strong linkage of ethical issues to SHEINâs Philippine market performance
Comprehensive integration in STEEPLE, TBL and financial analyses
weaknesses: Some sections (e.g., Technology) remain descriptive without explicit ethical lens
Occasional drift to global data without reconnecting to the ethical focus
Could more seamlessly weave ethics into every analytical paragraph
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work demonstrates a strong understanding of ethics as the key concept, with clear analytical thinking and substantial integration into the organization context. Minor lapsesâmainly in descriptive passages without an ethical critiqueâprevent full seamless integration, aligning with band-4 performance.

strengths: Four well-labelled supporting documents within the required range
Each document ties directly to a claim in the text
Documents supply relevant statistical and qualitative evidence
weaknesses: Depth is unevenâsome citations lack Philippine-specific data
Limited range of perspectives; a few sources remain generic
Supporting documents could include more diverse or locally nuanced materials
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the quantity of documents meets the top requirement, the depth and range of ideas fall below the âin-depth range of ideasâ standard. The evidence is relevant but unevenly deep, warranting a mid-band mark.

strengths: Clear selection of three standard HL tools (STEEPLE, TBL, financial statements)
Tools are generally applied to the research question
A well-labeled TBL diagram aids theoretical understanding
weaknesses: Toolkit is adequate but not especially varied or sophisticated
Application sometimes lapses into description without tying back to the RQ
Relevance of some tool applications is not consistently clear
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The selection and use of analytical tools are adequate but lack sophistication and consistent relevance. This performance aligns with band-2 (basic selection and superficial application) rather than higher bands.
feedback.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

strengths: Appropriate use of quantitative and qualitative data points
Good transparency around source limitations
Some effective links between data and evaluative claims
weaknesses: Data use lacks consistent Philippine specificity
Analysis occasionally remains surface-level and descriptive
Integration across analytical tools is uneven
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student shows basic data selection and conducts some analysis, but it is often superficial and not fully integrated. This equates to band-2 performance for analysis and evaluation.

strengths: Conclusions are fully consistent with the evidence presented
Explicitly and clearly answer the research question
Synthesizes key findings on market performance and long-term ethical risks
weaknesses: Could offer more concrete recommendations for ethical strategy
Synthesis is strong but stops short of actionable next steps
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The conclusion fully aligns with evidence and directly addresses the research question, meeting band-3 descriptors. The absence of specific strategic recommendations prevents a higher score.

strengths: Follows the IA format with clear headings and logical flow
Title page, contents, sections, and references are all present
Logical sequencing supports reader navigation
weaknesses: Table of contents lacks page numbers and numbering for headings
Minor structural lapses (e.g., cut-off sentence) interrupt flow
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The report exhibits clear organization throughout, satisfying band-2 criteria for structure. Small navigational and formatting issues prevent complete polish.

strengths: Includes all required presentation elements (RQ, candidate code field, word count, bibliography, citations)
Word count compliance and diagram labelling are excellent
weaknesses: Inconsistent citation formatting and incomplete bibliographic details
Candidate code field left blank; minor heading formatting inconsistencies
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All mandatory presentation elements are present, aligning with band-2 performance. Inconsistencies in referencing and minor formatting errors justify this top band mark for presentation.

strandSelections.reasoning: The report follows the required IA format (title page, table of contents, numbered headings, conclusion, references).  Logical sequencing supports reader navigation, warranting the highest available band for structure.

Each supporting document is directly tied to a claim in the text (e.g., thrifted fashion emissions, Public Eye labour investigation, Grist emissions data).  They are relevant and provide more than superficial detail, yet depth is unevenâsome statistics lack Philippine specificity and a couple of citations (e.g., Bangalore 2023) supply only generic commentary.  This fits ârelevant and sufficiently in-depthâ rather than the richer âprovide range of ideasâ requirement for the top band.

All IB-mandated presentation elements appear: title with RQ, candidate code, word count, contents page, citations, bibliography and an appropriately labelled diagram.  Therefore the work meets the top band for presentation.
Four clearly labelled supporting documents (SD1-SD4) are appended and fall within the required 3â5 range.  Hence the highest band for number of documents is met.

Tools are generally applied to the research question: STEEPLE links environmental factors to brand risk, TBL evaluates planet/people/profit trade-offs, and financial figures are interpreted in light of ethical controversies.  Nevertheless, sections sometimes lapse into description (e.g., listing COâ statistics without tying back to Philippine sales), meaning relevance is âadequately appliedâ but not consistently clear or effective.

The student uses multiple quantitative datapointsâdownload numbers, income figures, COâ tonnage, revenue/net income tableâand qualitative evidence.  These selections are appropriate but not always rigorously sourced to the Philippine context, and some numbers (e.g., valuation changes) are global, leaving gaps.  This aligns with âadequate selection and use.â

Analysis moves beyond description in placesâlinking price sensitivity to continued growth, or explaining how air-freight raises emissionsâyet integration across tools is uneven, and evaluation of long-term risk remains surface-level.  Thus the work shows âsome analysisâ rather than the âmostly effectiveâ standard required for band 4.
The research question is restated and directly answered in the concluding paragraph with a clear judgement.  This explicit response meets the highest descriptor.

The student selects three standard HL toolsâSTEEPLE, Triple Bottom Line and Financial Statementsâwhich cover external environment, sustainability and financial data.  This constitutes an adequate, IB-appropriate toolkit but not an especially varied or sophisticated mix, so the âadequate selectionâ band is most fitting.

The conclusion synthesises findings, stating that sustainability practices have âminimal impactâ on current market performance but pose âsignificant long-term risks,â a stance supported by earlier affordability data and ethical critiques.  Assertions are consistent with evidence provided, satisfying the top band for consistency.

SHEIN in the Philippine market is the explicit focus in every analytical tool, with Philippine download figures, family income data, second-hand fashion trends, and local competitors such as Bench and H&M referenced.  The discussion repeatedly relates global ethical issues back to possible effects on Philippine consumer behaviour and market share.  While comprehensive, the integration occasionally drifts to global data without reconnection, so the work reaches âstrong analysis with partial integrationâ rather than the fully seamless standard.

The student consistently frames the investigation around the key concept of Ethics, exploring labour abuses, carbon emissions, green-washing and price ethics in every major section (STEEPLE, TBL, Finance).  Ethical considerations are linked to market performanceâfor example, reputational risk among Gen-Z Filipino consumers and potential regulatory fines affecting profitability.  However, some passages remain largely descriptive (e.g., Technology subsection) and do not always draw out an explicit ethical lens, so integration is strong but not seamless throughout, aligning best with the âstrong understanding with partial integrationâ descriptor.

annotations.comment: The candidate code field is left blank; include your IB candidate code to ensure proper identification.
The research question is articulated clearly and concisely at the start, providing a strong foundation for the investigation.
Word count is within the 2,000-word limit; excellent adherence to IB requirements.
The table of contents lacks page numbers; add page references for each section to improve navigability.
Introduction heading lacks a corresponding page number; include page details in the ToC.
The section titles in the ToC are unnumbered; consider adding numbering to mirror in-text headings for clarity.
While the introduction provides context, an explicit link to the key concept of ethics would strengthen the integration from the outset.
The research question and choice of analytical tools are clearly stated; this frames the investigation effectively.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Acknowledging limitations of secondary sources is good practice; this transparency enhances the reportâs credibility.
The social analysis effectively uses supporting documents to highlight consumer backlash; strong application of data.
The use of global view counts is informative, but integrating Philippine-specific social media metrics would enhance the local relevance of the analysis.
The technology section is descriptive; explicitly apply the ethical lens to explain how these practices create ethical risks or trade-offs.
The discussion of affordability vs sustainability in the Philippines is relevant; well-linked to primary findings.
The sentence at the end of the economic section is cut off; ensure the analysis completes the thought and connects back to the research question.
The economic analysis could be strengthened by comparing average income to specific product prices to illustrate price elasticity.
Add analysis of how environmental factors influence purchasing behavior specifically in the Philippines rather than global percentages.
The legal section header uses inconsistent formatting; apply consistent heading levels throughout for professionalism.
The STEEPLE summary effectively synthesizes factors; well done linking short-term dynamics with long-term risks.
Introducing the Triple Bottom Line with a clear definition supports reader understanding; this is a solid theoretical application.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The TBL diagram is original and well-labeled; it effectively visualizes the interconnections of the three pillars.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Consider adding quantitative data to each TBL dimension, such as PH-specific metrics, to deepen your evaluation.
The Planet section references emissions targets but could integrate the ethical concept by discussing corporate responsibility commitments explicitly.
The Profit analysis connects ethics to profitability risks; this demonstrates clear evaluative thinking.
The Finance Statement introduction sets context well, but the transition to the table could be smoother by explicitly stating the analytical intent.
The table presents global data clearly but lacks a column for Philippine market figures; including local data would strengthen relevance.
The interpretation of financial trends is insightful, linking net income changes to legal and marketing costs; this reflects effective analytical depth.
The paragraph on Philippine sales is informative but would benefit from a clearer tie back to the research question on sustainability practices.
The conclusion heading is clear, but the first sentence has a fragmented phrase ("To conclude. fast-fashion market."); revise for readability.
The conclusion answers the research question and synthesizes findings, but could offer more concrete recommendations for SHEINâs ethical strategies.
References are listed, but citation formatting is inconsistent; apply a single citation style consistently throughout.
Several entries end abruptly or lack complete details (e.g., Balita 2024); ensure every reference includes full bibliographic information.
